mission.title: kyverno: [Feature] Show violations as warnings
mission.description: [Feature] Show violations as warnings. Requested by 25+ users.

steps.title: Check current kyverno deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kyverno version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
helm list -n kyverno 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kyverno installation.

steps.title: Review kyverno configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kyverno configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get configmap -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kyverno
```
Kubernetes 1.19 allows returning warnings in API responses:

https://kubernetes.io/blog/2020/09/03/warnings/?s=03

Perhaps we can use this to report policy violations?

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature] Show violations as warnings
steps.description:
## Related issue

## Proposed Changes

- Omitted the generation of events on blocked resources and policies to avoid extra API calls on blocked resources

### Proof Manifests
Logs:

Here we can see there are no extra API calls when a resource is blocked due to an enforce policy!

##
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: roles-dictionary
  namespace: default
data:
  allowed-roles: "[\"cluster-admin\", \"cluster-operator\", \"tenant-admin\"]"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get events -n kyverno --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] Show violations as warnings" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Related issue

## Proposed Changes

- Omitted the generation of events on blocked resources and policies to avoid extra API calls on blocked resources

### Proof Manifests
Logs:

Here we can see there are no extra API calls when a resource is blocked due to an enforce policy!

## Checklist
  
  

## Further Comments
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: roles-dictionary
  namespace: default
data:
  allowed-roles: "[\"cluster-admin\", \"cluster-operator\", \"tenant-admin\"]"

name: prepend-image-registry
policies:
  - prepend_image_registry.yaml
resources:
  - resource.yaml
variables: values.yaml
results:
  - policy: prepend-registry
    rule: prepend-registry-containers
    resource: mypod
    # if mutate rule
    patchedResource: patchedResource01.yaml
    kind: Pod
    result: pass

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kyverno installed or the issue environment reproducible.
